content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a meta-structure that can unify fragmented LLM self-reflection mechanisms by letting each new reflection perspective (like the inconsistent perspectives from Self-Contrast or multiple perspectives from Mirror) itself be iteratively self-refined. This creates a recursive reflection loop where not only the LLM's output gets refined, but the perspectives used to critique that output also improve over iterations without any additional supervised training data.
